title: Campaign Plan
description: P-B. Ordered milestone backlog for the Campaign loop — a multi-milestone extension of the Delivery Loop (Concept 2), NOT the Auto Loop (Constitution §1.7-E / §3.7). The Campaign runner iterates these milestones in deterministic topological order (depends_on; default linear), driving each through the existing single-sub-sprint Driver, and halts only at human-authority gates. archive/2026-06-20-autonomous-delivery-design.md §5.

campaign_id.description: Path-safe id (no separators, no leading dot) — interpolated into the campaign audit ledger filename; the runner rejects an unsafe value fail-closed.

goal.description: The top-level user-facing goal (以终为始) the campaign decomposes into the milestone backlog.

signed_by_human.description: P-B campaign-tier gate (campaign_plan_signoff): the Customer signs the milestone backlog before the runner drives it (design §5.1). The runner PAUSES at campaign_plan_signoff until this is true. Enforced by the campaign runner, not the charter validator.

budget.description: Campaign-level COUNTABLE caps (design §5.4a). $ cost is NOT a dimension — no adapter reports per-call cost and subscription harnesses do not expose it (charter budget.max_api_usd caveat). All OPTIONAL; an absent dimension is unbounded.

max_subsprints.description: Max sub-sprints the campaign may dispatch before campaign_budget_exhausted.

max_total_spawns.description: Max cumulative spawns (summed from each run_loop summary's spawn_count).

max_wall_clock_minutes.description: Max cumulative wall-clock minutes (measured via the injected clock).

merge_policy.description: Forward-compat seam for a future PARALLEL runner; the sequential runner ignores it.

isolation_strategy.description: Forward-compat seam; the sequential runner uses shared.

id.description: Path-safe milestone id (it + the sub-sprint id form the per-unit Driver run dir/loop_id).

acceptance_bar.description: One-line statement of what Acceptance must confirm at this milestone's close.

functional_acceptance.description: P-C. Per-milestone acceptance class. NO schema default (absence stays distinguishable from explicit static). Precedence in campaign.derive_milestone_context: an explicit value (incl. 'static') OVERRIDES; absent ⇒ INHERITS charter tooling.acceptance.functional.mode; else static. browser_e2e fires the orchestrator-owned browser evidence gate at this milestone's close. The projected {mode, source} is recorded in derived-context.json. archive/2026-06-20-pc-browser-e2e-design.md §4.2.

subsprint_sequence.description: Ordered (path-safe) sub-sprint ids, UNIQUE within a milestone (the follow-up id-novelty check + per-unit loop_id require it). May be authored up front (Deliver) or filled by the per-milestone full_chain_guided decompose (delivery-loop §4.2.4-G).

depends_on.description: Milestone ids that MUST complete before this one (deterministic topological order). Absent ⇒ linear backlog order. The sequential runner enforces order + lock non-overlap so a future parallel runner is sound.

module_locks.description: Forward-compat: resource paths this milestone owns, for a future parallel runner's isolation. The sequential runner records but does not contend on them.
